title: Delicate Steamed Brazilian Cherry Stew
description: This paleo-friendly stew features tender Brazilian cherries steamed in a cataplana vessel, creating a tangy and pillowy festive dish. It is perfect for special occasions and can be served as a dessert or snack.
instructions: Rinse the Brazilian cherries and remove any stems or leaves.
In a large bowl, mix together the coconut milk, honey, lime juice, grated ginger, and salt.
Add the Brazilian cherries to the bowl and toss to coat with the coconut milk mixture.
Place the cherry mixture in the cataplana vessel or large steamer basket.
Steam the cherry mixture over boiling water for 10 minutes, or until the cherries are tender.
Remove the cataplana vessel from the heat and let it cool for a few minutes.
Serve the stew warm, garnished with additional grated ginger if desired.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
